title: Puerto Rican Real Talk
description: Listen to authentic back-and-forth conversations with native Puerto Ricans, then practice natural responses.

responseExercise.prompt: Your Puerto Rican friend says: “¡Hola Cristina!” What’s a natural reply?

choices.feedback: Perfect — warm and very Puerto Rican.
Good, but a bit more formal than most PR convos.

conversationTips: Puerto Ricans often use first names right away — it feels warm and familiar.
The rising tone on “¡Hola!” shows excitement to see someone.

responseExercise.prompt: Your friend says they’ll see you later. Natural reply?

choices.feedback: Works, but “nos vemos” is more common in PR.

conversationTips: “Nos vemos” is the go-to casual goodbye on the island — way more common than “adiós”.

lines.speaker: Native
lines.en: Perfect, I’ll wait for the info, thanks.

responseExercise.prompt: They’re waiting for info. Natural reply?

conversationTips: Dropping the subject (“te la mando”) is very common and natural in spoken PR Spanish.

lines.speaker: Native
lines.es: Por favor, dame la información completa.
lines.en: Please give me the full information.

responseExercise.prompt: They want the full details. Reply naturally.

choices.feedback: Natural and direct — exactly how Puerto Ricans ask for and give info.

conversationTips: “Dame” + request is the everyday way Puerto Ricans ask for something without sounding rude.
These are real clips from Puerto Rican family/friend conversations — the rhythm and slang are 100% authentic.
Listen first, then repeat out loud (shadowing) — this is the fastest way to sound natural.
Puerto Ricans speak fast and drop syllables — that’s normal! The more you hear it, the more it feels like home.
